creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Jan Beich from comment #7)

I guess my point would have been better stated as: using
-stdlib=libc++ will not remove the need for use of the
likes of -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc11 (or whatever is
the proper match) in order to keep things working for
the likes of aarch64.

Nor will -stdlib=libc++ fix various examples that are
broken as things are, examples that lack such an rpath
definition: The __unordtf2@GCC_4.5.0 binding problem
(and related) make various builds fail last I knew,
sometimes not just via such references from libstdc++ .
